name: Vishwarupa Vishnu of Changu Narayan
description: Monumental stone sculpture showing Vishnu in his universal form (Vishwarupa) with multiple arms. One of the finest Lichhavi-era sculptures surviving in the Valley.

name: Paubha of Vasudhara
description: Scroll painting depicting Vasudhara, the Buddhist goddess of wealth and abundance. Classic example of Newar Paubha art with mineral pigments and gold leaf.

name: Kal Bhairav Stone Image
description: Massive stone sculpture of Kal Bhairav (fearsome form of Shiva) at Kathmandu Durbar Square. Believed to make liars who stand before it vomit blood.

name: Uma-Maheshwar Relief
description: Stone relief sculpture showing Shiva and Parvati seated together on Mount Kailash. A masterpiece of Lichhavi-era art. Multiple copies exist across the Valley.

name: Golden Taleju Image
description: Gold-plated copper image of Taleju Bhawani inside the main sanctum of Taleju Temple. Accessible only to the king (historically) and select priests.

name: Sleeping Vishnu of Budhanilkantha
description: Massive 5-meter reclining Vishnu sculpture lying in a recessed water tank. One of the largest stone sculptures in Nepal. The king of Nepal traditionally may not visit this site.

name: Paubha of Green Tara
description: Exquisite Newar scroll painting of Green Tara seated on a lotus. Features fine gold line work and rich mineral pigments. One of the finest surviving medieval Paubhas.
technique: Mineral pigments and gold on cotton
location_name: Los Angeles County Museum of Art

name: Garuda Statue at Changu Narayan
description: Kneeling Garuda figure facing the main Changu Narayan temple. Hands folded in namaste. Important Lichhavi-era metalwork showing early Newar bronze casting skills.
